title: nit: podman usage: one of our dashes is missing
body:
This has been bugging me for weeks; I've now changed my mind about it being too unimportant to file.

podman error/usage messages display long option names with only one dash:
```
# podman ps --last f                            
Incorrect Usage: invalid value "f" for flag -last: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "f": invalid syntax
```
This is jarring because `-last` is not an option: it must be explicitly given as `--last` with two dashes.

This happens with *every* usage message about incorrect options. I'm hoping it's a trivial fix to a golang arg-processing call.

comments.body: @mheon, smells like a urfave/cli hiccup.  I'll take a quick peak.

Let the debate begin:
```
$ docker ps --last -f name=foo
invalid argument "-f" for --last: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "-f": invalid syntax
See 'docker ps --help'.
$
```
Definitely a bug and should be fixed. Most likely i urfave/cli
Removed short option handling to see if it could have been us that introduced the error, but error still appears.
i wonder if we should vndr in the latest code and see if that fixes it?
@baude Sure. If that doesn't resolve it, we can open an issue upstream.
I took a quick look at their code, and I can not even figure where this error is coming from.  But we have the same issue on buildah.

It looks like the error generation while parsing flags is responsible: https://github.com/urfave/cli/blob/master/command.go#L128

I have some time to spare and can look into this (seems to affect too many tools to ignore).

It turns out that `urfave/cli` partially depends on the flag package from the stdlib, which uses single dashes for flags. You can find the error coming from here: https://golang.org/src/flag/flag.go#L912
Any thoughts on ways to fix this?
